items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` **Service directory:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` **Triggered by:** PR #58483 by `@ArcturusZhang` ## What Changed PR #58483 introduced the new `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` 1.0.0-beta.1 package. It added support for four `Microsoft.ServiceNetworking` resources: - `TrafficController` (Application Gateway for Containers) - `TrafficControllerFrontend` - `TrafficControllerAssociation` - `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` (WAF security policy sub-resource) ## Gaps Found - `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is listed in the CHANGELOG as a key added resource but has **no example in the README** and **no test-backed snippet** — the README only demonstrates the first three resource types - The README `## Examples` section lacks a snippet showing how to attach a WAF security policy to a Traffic Controller via `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.WafPolicyId` - The `## Authenticate the Client` section heading exists but has no content (minor, consistent with other Provisioning packages but worth noting) - The `## Next steps` section is empty — should at minimum link to the Azure Application Gateway for Containers docs and related provisioning packages
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File 1:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` Action: **Edit** — add a new test method with a snippet region for the security policy resource. Add the following method after `CreateTrafficController()`: ```csharp internal static Trycep CreateSecurityPolicyTest() { return new Trycep().Define( ctx => { #region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy Infrastructure infra = new(); TrafficController controller = new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01); infra.Add(controller); ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy securityPolicy = new(nameof(securityPolicy), ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01) { Parent = controller, WafPolicyId = new ResourceIdentifier("/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.Network/applicationGatewayWebApplicationFirewallPolicies/myWafPolicy"), }; infra.Add(securityPolicy); #endregion return infra; }); } [Test] public async Task CreateSecurityPolicy() { await using Trycep test = CreateSecurityPolicyTest(); await test.CompareAsync(); } ``` **File 2:** `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` Action: **Edit** — add a new example section and snippet placeholder after the existing "Create a Traffic Controller" example, and fill in the empty sections. In `## Examples`, append after the closing ` ``` ` of the existing snippet: ```markdown ### Attach a WAF Security Policy to a Traffic Controller This example demonstrates how to associate a WAF security policy with an Application Gateway for Containers Traffic Controller using `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`. ```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy ``` ``` Replace the empty `### Authenticate the Client` section with: ```markdown ### Authenticate the Client `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` is used for **infrastructure definition** (generating Bicep templates), not for runtime service calls, so no Azure credential is required at definition time. To deploy the generated infrastructure, use [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` Replace the empty `## Next steps` section with: ```markdown ## Next steps - [Azure Application Gateway for Containers overview]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) - [Azure.Provisioning overview](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/provisioning/Azure.Provisioning/README.md) - [Azure Developer CLI (azd)]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Write the sample code in the test file** (done in Step 1 above): - File: `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/tests/BasicServiceNetworkingTests.cs` - Snippet name: `ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy` - Wrap the infrastructure definition code with `#region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy` / `#endregion` 2. **Add a snippet placeholder in the README** (done in Step 1 above): - Location: `## Examples` section, after the existing "Create a Traffic Controller" example - Placeholder: ```` ```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingSecurityPolicy ``` ```` - After running `Update-Snippets.ps1`, the content between the fences will be replaced with the code from the test file ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md` currently contains these sections. Status: 1. ✅ **Getting started** — present - ✅ Install the package - ✅ Prerequisites - ⚠️ Authenticate the Client — heading exists but is empty; add content (see Step 1) 2. ✅ **Key concepts** — present (minimal but consistent with other Provisioning packages) 3. ⚠️ **Examples** — present but missing `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` example (see Step 1) 4. ✅ **Troubleshooting** — present 5. ⚠️ **Next steps** — heading exists but is empty; add links (see Step 1) 6. ✅ **Contributing** — present ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next: 1. `dotnet build sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 servicenetworking` — injects snippet code from test `#region` tags into README placeholders; check `git diff` afterward to confirm README was updated 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 servicenetworking` — regenerates public API listing files (no public API changes expected here, but run to be safe) 5. `dotnet format sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/` — ensures consistent code formatting
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
